draft.text:
## Opening

I try to write about my work the way I think about it — plainly, and grounded in what I actually built or measured. I am Alex Rivera, applying to the MS in Computer Science - Machine Learning Specialization at Georgia Institute of Technology for Fall 2026. The program's concentration in Machine Learning and its core-fit curriculum match where I want to spend the next two years. Georgia Institute of Technology is where this work lives at the scale I need. I want two focused years to go from being an enthusiastic undergraduate researcher to someone who can own a non-trivial research project end to end.

## Research background

I completed my Bachelor of Science in Computer Science at University of Georgia. My cumulative GPA was 3.82 / 4.00.

As an undergraduate researcher in the UGA NLP Lab I led a retrieval-augmented code search project that asked a narrow question: does span-level supervision meaningfully improve retrieval on low-resource code repositories? I built the evaluation harness, designed an ablation... Led retrieval-augmented code search project end to end as an undergraduate researcher at the UGA NLP Lab. Designed an ablation over span granularity and built the evaluation harness.

I built a multi-agent reinforcement learning environment that modeled a shared household pantry as a coordination game between independent restocking agents. The interesting part was not the reward function; it was the failure mode. Two agents with almost identical policies... Implemented a multi-agent RL environment in PettingZoo and PyTorch around a pantry-restocking coordination game. Diagnosed a free-rider failure mode and ran an ablation comparing no-communication baselines against a cheap communication channel.

## Why this program

The MS in Computer Science - Machine Learning Specialization is structured around the coursework and research I want to be inside of: strong:machine learning, strong:deep learning, strong:computer vision, strong:natural language processing. My own focus areas — natural language processing, multi-agent reinforcement learning, on-device inference — map directly onto that menu. I am specifically interested in working with Director of Graduate Programs, School of Computer Science, whose research in machine learning, natural language processing overlaps with the problems I want to work on. Their published work in machine learning is part of what convinced me the fit was real.

## Closing

I am also applying to the Graduate Research Assistantship - Machine Learning Center, which (per its posting) supports this kind of work and is essential to my plan. I want the next two years to be focused and checkable, and I want advisors who make room for scope-limited experiments over broad, thin projects. I am applying for MS in Computer Science / Artificial Intelligence track and I am ready to contribute to the lab on day one.

draft.title: Resume tailoring — MS in Computer Science - Machine Learning Specialization @ Georgia Institute of Technology

claims.text: # Alex Rivera
alex.rivera@example.com
Bachelor of Science in Computer Science, University of Georgia (2025) — GPA 3.82 / 4.00.
Tailored for the MS in Computer Science - Machine Learning Specialization at Georgia Institute of Technology.
Emphasis: natural language processing, multi-agent reinforcement learning, on-device inference.
Secondary emphasis: alignment with Director of Graduate Programs, School of Computer Science (machine learning, natural language processing).

- **Retrieval-augmented code search in the UGA NLP Lab.** As an undergraduate researcher in the UGA NLP Lab I led a retrieval-augmented code search project that asked a narrow question: does span-level supervision meaningfully improve retrieval on low-resource code repositories? I built the evaluation harness, designed an ablation...
- Led retrieval-augmented code search project end to end as an undergraduate researcher at the UGA NLP Lab.
- Designed an ablation over span granularity and built the evaluation harness.
- Authored a span-level error analysis that became part of a workshop paper contribution.

- **Multi-agent RL for pantry restocking.** I built a multi-agent reinforcement learning environment that modeled a shared household pantry as a coordination game between independent restocking agents. The interesting part was not the reward function; it was the failure mode. Two agents with almost identical policies...
- Implemented a multi-agent RL environment in PettingZoo and PyTorch around a pantry-restocking coordination game.
- Diagnosed a free-rider failure mode and ran an ablation comparing no-communication baselines against a cheap communication channel.
- Reported that coordination collapsed without communication and throughput improved modestly with it.

draft.text:
## Header

# Alex Rivera alex.rivera@example.com

## Education

Bachelor of Science in Computer Science, University of Georgia (2025) — GPA 3.82 / 4.00.

## Tailoring summary

Tailored for the MS in Computer Science - Machine Learning Specialization at Georgia Institute of Technology. Emphasis: natural language processing, multi-agent reinforcement learning, on-device inference. Secondary emphasis: alignment with Director of Graduate Programs, School of Computer Science (machine learning, natural language processing).

## Selected experience & projects

- **Retrieval-augmented code search in the UGA NLP Lab.** As an undergraduate researcher in the UGA NLP Lab I led a retrieval-augmented code search project that asked a narrow question: does span-level supervision meaningfully improve retrieval on low-resource code repositories? I built the evaluation harness, designed an ablation... - Led retrieval-augmented code search project end to end as an undergraduate researcher at the UGA NLP Lab. - Designed an ablation over span granularity and built the evaluation harness. - Authored a span-level error analysis that became part of a workshop paper contribution.

- **Multi-agent RL for pantry restocking.** I built a multi-agent reinforcement learning environment that modeled a shared household pantry as a coordination game between independent restocking agents. The interesting part was not the reward function; it was the failure mode. Two agents with almost identical policies... - Implemented a multi-agent RL environment in PettingZoo and PyTorch around a pantry-restocking coordination game. - Diagnosed a free-rider failure mode and ran an ablation comparing no-communication baselines against a cheap communication channel. - Reported that coordination collapsed without communication and throughput improved modestly with it.

claims.text: I write about my own work the way I think about it: one argument per paragraph, nothing said that cannot be shown.
My name is Alex Rivera, and my path to the MS in Computer Science - Machine Learning Specialization started with small, concrete projects that forced me to be specific about what I actually changed.
I trained at University of Georgia, and the projects that stuck with me were the ones where I had to defend the choices I made.

As an undergraduate researcher in the UGA NLP Lab I led a retrieval-augmented code search project that asked a narrow question: does span-level supervision meaningfully improve retrieval on low-resource code repositories? I built the evaluation harness, designed an ablation...
Led retrieval-augmented code search project end to end as an undergraduate researcher at the UGA NLP Lab.
I want two focused years to go from being an enthusiastic undergraduate researcher to someone who can own a non-trivial research project end to end.
The next two years are about focused work on natural language processing, multi-agent reinforcement learning, on-device inference.
The MS in Computer Science - Machine Learning Specialization is where that kind of scoped, checkable work lives.
That is why I am applying to Georgia Institute of Technology.

draft.text:
## Personal statement

I write about my own work the way I think about it: one argument per paragraph, nothing said that cannot be shown. My name is Alex Rivera, and my path to the MS in Computer Science - Machine Learning Specialization started with small, concrete projects that forced me to be specific about what I actually changed.

I trained at University of Georgia, and the projects that stuck with me were the ones where I had to defend the choices I made. As an undergraduate researcher in the UGA NLP Lab I led a retrieval-augmented code search project that asked a narrow question: does span-level supervision meaningfully improve retrieval on low-resource code repositories? I built the evaluation harness, designed an ablation... Led retrieval-augmented code search project end to end as an undergraduate researcher at the UGA NLP Lab. I want two focused years to go from being an enthusiastic undergraduate researcher to someone who can own a non-trivial research project end to end.

The next two years are about focused work on natural language processing, multi-agent reinforcement learning, on-device inference. The MS in Computer Science - Machine Learning Specialization is where that kind of scoped, checkable work lives. That is why I am applying to Georgia Institute of Technology.

draft.title: Cover letter — Graduate Research Assistantship - Machine Learning Center

claims.text: Dear Elena Park,
I am writing in plain sentences because I want the ask to be easy to check.
I am applying to the Graduate Research Assistantship - Machine Learning Center at Georgia Institute of Technology.
I am Alex Rivera, a candidate for the MS in Computer Science - Machine Learning Specialization starting Fall 2026.
The MS in Computer Science - Machine Learning Specialization concentration is Machine Learning, which is what made this funding route the right one to pursue.
I am specifically interested in working with the Director of Graduate Programs, School of Computer Science on machine learning, natural language processing.

As an undergraduate researcher in the UGA NLP Lab I led a retrieval-augmented code search project that asked a narrow question: does span-level supervision meaningfully improve retrieval on low-resource code repositories? I built the evaluation harness, designed an ablation...
Led retrieval-augmented code search project end to end as an undergraduate researcher at the UGA NLP Lab.
I have read the group's public work and it is directly connected to the problem I just described.
I would like to be considered for this assistantship in tandem with my MS in Computer Science - Machine Learning Specialization application.
I can commit to the funding's time expectation as described on the posting.
I can be reached at alex.rivera@example.com.
Thank you for considering this application for MS in Computer Science / Artificial Intelligence track.

draft.text:
Dear Elena Park,

I am writing in plain sentences because I want the ask to be easy to check. I am applying to the Graduate Research Assistantship - Machine Learning Center at Georgia Institute of Technology. I am Alex Rivera, a candidate for the MS in Computer Science - Machine Learning Specialization starting Fall 2026. The MS in Computer Science - Machine Learning Specialization concentration is Machine Learning, which is what made this funding route the right one to pursue.

I am specifically interested in working with the Director of Graduate Programs, School of Computer Science on machine learning, natural language processing. As an undergraduate researcher in the UGA NLP Lab I led a retrieval-augmented code search project that asked a narrow question: does span-level supervision meaningfully improve retrieval on low-resource code repositories? I built the evaluation harness, designed an ablation... Led retrieval-augmented code search project end to end as an undergraduate researcher at the UGA NLP Lab. I have read the group's public work and it is directly connected to the problem I just described.

I would like to be considered for this assistantship in tandem with my MS in Computer Science - Machine Learning Specialization application. I can commit to the funding's time expectation as described on the posting. I can be reached at alex.rivera@example.com. Thank you for considering this application for MS in Computer Science / Artificial Intelligence track.
